message:
feat(widgets): add Fenwick tree variable height tracking to Virtualized

Extend the Virtualized widget with O(log n) Fenwick tree-based height
tracking for improved performance with large lists that have variable
item heights.

New API:
- with_variable_heights_fenwick(default_height, capacity) builder
  - Creates ItemHeight::VariableFenwick variant
  - Enables O(log n) scroll-to-index mapping
  - More efficient than Variable(HeightCache) for large lists

visible_count() implementation for VariableFenwick:
- Delegates to tracker.visible_count(scroll_offset, viewport_height)
- Returns accurate count using Fenwick prefix sums
- Avoids O(visible) iteration of Variable variant

find_item_at_offset() refinements:
- Improved documentation of offset-to-item mapping invariants
- Clarified that item i occupies [offset_of_item(i), offset_of_item(i+1))
- Fixed edge case handling for offset == 0 (separate branch)
- Enhanced comments explaining Fenwick tree prefix sum semantics

Performance characteristics:
- Variable(HeightCache): O(visible) for visible_count
- VariableFenwick: O(log n) for visible_count
- Recommended for lists with 1000+ items and variable heights

Part of bd-2zbk (Virtualized List optimization epic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
